message:
[MED] fcp-core: close past-expiry revocation suppression attack

RevocationRegistry::add_revocation only compared effective_at and
replaced the stored entry whenever the incoming one started earlier.
That rule closed the "defer into the far future" suppression path,
but left a symmetric hole: an attacker with owner-key access (or a
replay window) could inject a revocation whose effective_at is
earlier than an active open-ended entry but whose expires_at is
already in the past — the earlier effective_at wins, the new entry
replaces the permanent one, and is_revoked_at(now) flips back to
false because "now" is past the replay's expires_at. The object is
then effectively un-revoked without any owner consent.

Tighten the replacement rule to "strictly dominates": replace only
when the incoming revocation starts no later AND ends no sooner
(None on expires_at treated as +∞). This preserves the existing
far-future deferral defense, keeps the first-writer-wins tie-break
on equal effective_at, and adds two regression tests covering both
the new suppression path and the positive "upgrade to permanent"
case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
